answers.a: A series where each number is the sum of the previous two numbers
answers.c: A series of even numbers only
answers.d: A series where each number is multiplied by 2

explanations.a: The Fibonacci series is defined such that each term is the sum of the two preceding terms.
explanations.b: Prime numbers are unrelated to Fibonacci series.
explanations.c: Fibonacci numbers include both even and odd numbers.
explanations.d: This describes a geometric progression, not Fibonacci.

questions.question: Which of the following are the first two numbers of the Fibonacci series?

explanations.a: This is not the standard starting point of Fibonacci series.
explanations.b: The Fibonacci series commonly starts with 0 and 1.
explanations.c: These values do not form a Fibonacci sequence.
explanations.d: These numbers are incorrect for Fibonacci series.

questions.question: Which programming concept allows a function to call itself?

explanations.a: Iteration uses loops, not self-calling functions.
explanations.b: Looping repeats code but does not involve function self-calls.
explanations.c: Recursion is a technique where a function calls itself.
explanations.d: Compilation translates code into machine language.

questions.question: Which loop is commonly used to generate the Fibonacci series iteratively?

explanations.a: Conditional statements do not generate sequences.
explanations.b: Switch cases are not used for sequence generation.
explanations.c: For and while loops are commonly used for iterative Fibonacci generation.
explanations.d: Do-while can be used, but it is not the only option.

questions.question: Which approach generally executes faster for large Fibonacci numbers?

explanations.a: Recursive Fibonacci is slower due to repeated calculations.
explanations.b: Iterative Fibonacci avoids repeated computations and is faster.
explanations.d: Compiler optimizations do not eliminate recursive overhead.
